cardiovascularRisk.name: Cardiovascular Risk Calculator
cardiovascularRisk.description: Estimate your 10-year risk of heart attack or stroke (Framingham).

meta.title: Cardiovascular Risk Calculator — Framingham 10-Year Risk
meta.description: Estimate your 10-year risk of heart attack and stroke with the Framingham general CVD score. Includes heart age, risk band and recommendations — free, anonymous.

cardiovascularRisk.title: Cardiovascular Risk Calculator
cardiovascularRisk.description: Estimate your 10-year risk of cardiovascular disease using the Framingham general CVD model.
cardiovascularRisk.tenYearLabel: Estimated 10-year risk of heart attack, stroke or cardiovascular event.
cardiovascularRisk.heartAgeOlder: Your heart is {years} years older than your actual age.
cardiovascularRisk.heartAgeYounger: Your heart is {years} years younger than your actual age.
cardiovascularRisk.advice_low: Low 10-year risk. Maintain: don't smoke, exercise regularly, follow a Mediterranean diet. Routine check-up every 4–6 years.
cardiovascularRisk.advice_borderline: Borderline risk. Intensify lifestyle: 150 min/week aerobic exercise, weight management, cholesterol and blood pressure check every 1–2 years.
cardiovascularRisk.advice_intermediate: Intermediate risk. Discuss with your physician: consider statin eligibility, optimize blood pressure, screen for diabetes, increase activity.
cardiovascularRisk.advice_high: High risk. Medical evaluation recommended — statin therapy, blood pressure control and lifestyle changes are usually indicated. ESC Guidelines 2021.
cardiovascularRisk.howItWorksText: This calculator uses the D'Agostino general CVD Framingham model (Circulation 2008). Inputs: age, sex, total and HDL cholesterol, systolic blood pressure (treated/untreated), smoking, diabetes. Validated for ages 30–79. Output: 10-year probability of myocardial infarction, stroke, heart failure or peripheral artery disease.
cardiovascularRisk.disclaimer: This calculator does not replace medical diagnosis. If your risk is elevated, or you have chest pain, shortness of breath or a strong family history, see your physician or cardiologist promptly.

faq.a: It estimates the probability of a cardiovascular event (heart attack, stroke, heart failure, peripheral artery disease) within the next 10 years. The figure is based on statistical models from large prospective cohort studies.

faq.q: How does Framingham differ from SCORE2?
faq.a: Framingham (US, 2008) covers fatal and non-fatal events for ages 30–79. SCORE2 (Europe, ESC 2021) was recalibrated for European populations and is the official ESC recommendation for ages 40–69. Both produce comparable risk estimates.

Heart age is the chronological age of someone with optimal risk factors who has the same cardiovascular risk as you. A heart age above your real age signals avoidable risk through lifestyle or therapy.

Total cholesterol: < 190 mg/dL (4.9 mmol/L). LDL: < 116 mg/dL (3.0 mmol/L) — much lower in high-risk patients. HDL: men > 40 mg/dL, women > 48 mg/dL. Triglycerides: < 150 mg/dL. Source: ESC/EAS Guidelines 2019.

AHA/ACC and ESC guidelines discuss statin therapy at a 10-year risk ≥ 7.5 % (intermediate) and generally recommend it at ≥ 20 %. The final decision is shared with the physician based on individual factors.

faq.q: How can I lower my risk?
faq.a: Quitting smoking halves cardiovascular risk within 5 years. 150 min/week of moderate exercise halves heart attack risk. Mediterranean diet (PREDIMED): –30 %. Lowering systolic BP by 10 mmHg: –20 %. Lowering LDL by 1 mmol/L: –22 % events.
